features_added: Review-Gate for key-position selection after Stockfish analysis
Plan mode with heuristic or LLM-backed task planning
BackgroundAgent slash workflow and benchmark event recorder
Runtime deployment script hardening and secret-safe Kimi ecosystem skill
Slash-to-Skill contract: 25 built-in tools as manifest-based skills
Structured legal-move truth and Stockfish quality bands for all legal moves
PTG live verification against the router with legal-move and board-truth context in downstream artifacts
Normalized RAG provenance for agent and researcher flows
OCR/FEN validity gates and analysis/training quality gates beyond PTG
Profile export and confirmed profile deletion
Privacy-safe RAG/researcher source_quality metrics in Search goldset and benchmark exports
Model-role benchmark feedback now shows model question, full prompts within import limits, live RAG context and full answer payloads within MEDIUMTEXT limits
Benchmark feedback agent can process automatically assessable open observations through the protected website API
CaiLama-Search supports the current SVW/DSB DWZ v2 ZIP export and enriches players with club and association data
CaiLama-Search source registry now carries rights and access metadata for open, official, commercial and UGC-sensitive sources

summary.name: CaiLama
summary.type: chess analysis, training, automation and retrieval ecosystem
summary.purpose: PGN import, Stockfish analysis, LLM-assisted commentary, DGT-aware training, knowledge search, player development and reproducible artifacts
summary.master_rule: CaiLama-Master is documentation, coordination and website only; it is not a monorepo and contains no runtime implementation from subprojects.
summary.positioning: CaiLama is a training workshop for serious chess improvement: PGN to grounded analysis, key positions, training cards, review feedback and repeatable artifacts.

product_positioning.primary_audience: club players, trainers and ambitious self-learners
core_loop: ground analysis with Stockfish and board facts
store valid PGN and training JSON artifacts
repeat through CLI, agent or DGT-aware workflows
feed review results back into priorities
quality_bar: annotated PGN roundtrip for training comments
Stockfish or heuristic grounding for evaluations
no guessed FEN output from OCR diagrams

families: Search/RAG recall, MRR, zero-hit-rate, latency and source quality
Router latency, fallback behavior, error handling and usage metrics
Analysis/PTG PGN validity, key positions, card quality, redundancy and review effect
OCR/FEN recognition quality and false-positive FEN prevention
Model-role feedback with duration, input/thinking/output tokens, quality, task success, logic errors and A/B preference
data_rules: no secrets, tokens, private paths or production credentials
prefer synthetic, public, explicitly approved or anonymized data
keep test, eval and later training data separated
no full private game archives or raw prompt/response logs in benchmark artifacts
benchmarks.specialized_llm_training: Later lever only after benchmark baselines, data governance, eval splits and router-compatible deployment.

repositories.name: CaiLama-Master
repositories.role: ecosystem documentation, website, roadmap, orchestration and local checks
rules: Do not store secrets, tokens, .env files or production credentials.
Keep runtime implementation out of the master repository.

repositories.name: CaiLama
repositories.role: main chess system for analysis, training, profiles, agent CLI, PGN, Stockfish and DGT-aware workflows
current_focus: DB hybrid access is configurable through database.access_mode values native, api and hybrid
bounded Webspace DB API status client uses protected POST /api/v1/status without SQL-over-HTTP or secret output
provider-side dump import is available through no-query/no-body append/reset endpoints that process a configured server-side .sql or .sql.gz file
provider database schemas are applied through protected PHP admin endpoints on the webspace, not through local provider DB access
PTG queue enqueue is wired; optional --run-llm-stages now connects game-flow/key-position analysis before personalized card generation
PTG product-loop artifacts are generated offline: source.pgn, flow_analysis.json, annotated.pgn, training.json schema 1.2 and quality_gates.json
Agent/DGT-aware training card retrieval, ReplayPosition.card_id and review stats in training prioritization are implemented
deterministic card scoring, mistake taxonomy and card types are implemented
Review-Gate accepts session directories and direct review_decision.json file paths
Review-Gate console flow and AgentLoop plan cascade are implemented
BackgroundAgent supports persistent slash tasks and synchronous cancellation for deterministic status/tests
BenchmarkEvent/BenchmarkStore provide secret-free duration and token metrics for model-role feedback
BoardTruth and evaluate_legal_moves expose structured tags, Stockfish score, engine rank and quality band for all legal moves
PTG live verification against the router is implemented and keeps legal-move plus board-truth context in PTG, review, coach and benchmark artifacts
RAG provenance is normalized for agent and researcher prompts with source/title, URL or origin, freshness, usage and uncertainty
OCR/FEN gates include false-positive prevention and FEN validity; analysis and training quality gates extend beyond PTG
profile export and confirmed profile deletion are implemented; retention/profile binding for file-based training cards and review histories remains follow-up
internal SearchAdapter is standard path for web_search and search_dwz
search_rag is suggested for research/source questions
DWZ identity linking is integrated in store and CLI
model-role benchmark events should expose duration and token values for protected website feedback
constraints: No live external, engine, router, database or hardware calls unless explicitly requested.
Do not expose local configuration secrets.
New domain logic should be implemented as reusable modules first.

repositories.name: CaiLama-LLM-Router
repositories.role: OpenAI-compatible LLM router with aliases, backend routing, fallback policies and operational checks
current_focus: streaming backend errors are returned as final SSE chunks
optional config hot reload through runtime.reload_config_on_request
validated backend-specific alias model mapping
JSON and Prometheus-compatible metrics output
cooldown metrics count only actual backend cooldown transitions
mypy src is clean
backend API key forwarding is implemented
privacy-safe token usage metrics, usage CLI, benchmark export and generic endpoint_path are implemented
coding-agent metrics remain separate from chess-role metrics; Kimi local work uses kimi-k2.6:cloud while Gemma4 remains only a chess-role candidate
specialized future models must remain behind the generic router contract
constraints: No chess product logic in the router.
No provider secrets in repository files.
Retrieval context belongs to CaiLama and CaiLama-Search.

repositories.name: CaiLama-Search
repositories.role: search, DWZ and RAG service with FastAPI, Meilisearch, crawler and import paths
current_focus: CaiLama-compatible API contract with normalized items, results, context and sources
explicit Meilisearch master key and dedicated API key in Docker defaults
admin service token via environment or localhost fallback
crawler source policy, robots groups, source validation and reindex tracking are tested
privacy-safe search observability endpoint is available
synthetic goldsets for search contract, DWZ search and RAG context are versioned and CLI-validated
localhost-protected goldset seeding for isolated test Meilisearch instances is available
goldset end-to-end smoke is automated through a local Docker Meilisearch instance, synthetic seeding and a scheduler-disabled API subprocess
optional semantic retrieval is implemented, default-off and guarded by lexical fallback
job orchestration with CaiLama queue and training
benchmark metrics, master-compatible benchmark export, RAG provenance and data policy are implemented
privacy-safe RAG/researcher source_quality metrics cover provenance, source count, unique domain count, freshness signals and source types without exporting URLs or domains
Docker-capable lexical versus hybrid benchmark comparison is documented; filter+hybrid and grouped multi-index response fixes are complete with pass rate 1.0 in both modes
DWZ staging verification is implemented as offline ZIP/CSV checks without import or network access
current SVW/DSB DWZ v2 live import is verified; player rows, club rows and association rows are joined so name, club and association searches work
verified live DWZ search forms include baublies, baublies, torsten, torsten baublies and Ratinger Schachklub

sources.yaml includes open core sources such as Wikibooks Opening Theory, Stockfish and Lc0 plus disabled rights-sensitive candidates such as Lichess Studies, Chess.com, ChessBase, 365Chess and Saint Louis
crawler source metadata now preserves source_license, access_mode, usage_policy, country_scope, ugc_level, annotation_level, rights_reviewed and tags
semantic.enabled remains false by default until a larger eval shows product benefit
constraints: No real Meilisearch keys, master keys, admin keys or .env files in repository files.
Live imports, crawls and external network calls require explicit instruction.
Do not rely on disabled tokenized DWZ interfaces.
Disabled or rights-sensitive sources must not become fulltext or training corpora before robots, license and product usage are reviewed.

interfaces.purpose: LLM access through OpenAI-compatible endpoints and role aliases
controlled search, RAG context and DWZ data
contract_notes: POST /v1/search is canonical; GET /v1/search?q=... remains compatible.
POST /v1/context accepts query parameters or JSON with query and limit.
Search responses expose native hits and normalized items/results.
Context responses expose context_blocks plus compatible context/sources.
DWZ search is populated from the current v2 bulk export and can expose player, club and association context.
RAG source records can carry rights and access metadata so open sources, official references and restricted sources stay distinguishable.

interfaces.purpose: provider database access through a narrow PHP API, server-side CaiLama dump imports and website login in single-database mode
contract_notes: No SQL-over-HTTP endpoints.
Real credentials belong only in private webspace config outside /public.
databases.cailama is the only PDO configuration in webspace single-database mode.
web_users and application tables share the provider database while remaining logically separated by table names.
Benchmark feedback uses cailama_model_benchmark_cases, cailama_model_benchmark_observations and cailama_model_feedback in databases.cailama.

Benchmark observations can be imported with benchmark:write or admin scope and contain only secret-free run metrics, model question, full prompts within import limits, optional task/position/error context, artifact references and full answer payloads within MEDIUMTEXT limits.
Benchmark cases keep role and task separate so candidate models can be compared per role instead of only globally.
The PTG three-game model benchmark emits probes for all CaiLama roles: router, small, large, task, translator, coach, analyst, critic, vision, scribe and researcher.
Imported benchmark observations are shown as blind candidate codes in the website feedback form; model names stay server-side.
benchmark-feedback-results.php shows protected blind aggregations by run, role, case and candidate.
There is no public registration endpoint; website users are created directly in web_users.
Status requires POST /api/v1/status with a Bearer token scope; there is no public GET status with API or database details.
The import endpoints accept no query parameters and no request body; authentication is through a Bearer token scope.
Provider schema endpoints accept no query parameters and no request body; authentication requires the admin scope.
Status, append import, reset import and admin use separate keys/scopes.
Reset import requires db_import:reset or admin.
Real webspace config is deployed outside the public webroot under cailama-private/api/config.local.php.
Large dump data is uploaded out-of-band to a non-public webspace folder and processed from the configured server-side file.
Missing import files are rejected and successfully imported files are deleted.
Reset imports require an explicit local allow_reset setting.
web/api_app/config.local.sample.php is a placeholder template only.

interfaces.purpose: documentation, coordination and local status checks only

now: CaiLama DB hybrid hardening after configurable native/api/hybrid access, bounded API status client, single-database webspace mode and server-side provider dump import endpoints
Training workshop focus on PGN-to-training-task loop instead of social platform features
PTG card retrieval and review prioritization after offline artifact generation
CaiLama deterministic scoring, mistake taxonomy and card types are implemented
DWZ identity linking and normalized RAG provenance are implemented in the current agent/researcher paths
Search benchmark comparison lexical versus hybrid is documented; correctness fixes, DWZ v2 live import and source registry metadata are complete; hybrid remains default-off pending product decision
Router infrastructure hardening is complete with backend API key forwarding, privacy-safe usage metrics, usage CLI and benchmark export
Model-role hypothesis is documented as a benchmark matrix and protected website feedback stores reusable quality data plus imported role-specific run observations in the provider database
next: CaiLama retention/profile binding for file-based training cards and review histories
human review of imported PTG model benchmark observations in the protected website feedback flow
Unified job orchestration for CaiLama and Search
Benchmark framework in Master for cross-repo measurements, website feedback, router usage metrics and result documentation
Search semantic production decision on a larger eval
Search source-policy gate for rights-sensitive and UGC-heavy candidates
later: unified job orchestration for import, crawl, game analysis, PTG and reindex
specialized LLM training only after data governance, benchmark baselines and eval splits
expansion: semantic retrieval release decision in CaiLama-Search after filter and grouped-response fixes
